title: Technology
description: Modern tech vocabulary for digital communication

vocabulary.meaning: The amount of data a network can transmit
vocabulary.example: We need more bandwidth for video calls.

vocabulary.meaning: A newer version of software or an app
vocabulary.example: Please install the latest security update.

vocabulary.meaning: A secret word used to access an account
vocabulary.example: Use a strong password with letters and numbers.

vocabulary.meaning: To copy a file from the internet to your device
vocabulary.example: Download the app from the App Store.

vocabulary.meaning: To send a file from your device to the internet
vocabulary.example: Please upload the document to Google Drive.

vocabulary.meaning: A message alert from an app
vocabulary.example: Turn off notifications during meetings.

vocabulary.meaning: A captured image of your screen
vocabulary.example: Take a screenshot and send it to me.

vocabulary.meaning: A copy of data stored for safety
vocabulary.example: Always backup your important files.

vocabulary.meaning: To find and fix problems in a system
vocabulary.example: Let me troubleshoot this connection issue.

vocabulary.meaning: Storing data on internet servers
vocabulary.example: My photos are saved in cloud storage.

vocabulary.meaning: A mobile phone with internet and computer capabilities
vocabulary.example: Most people carry a smartphone these days.

vocabulary.meaning: A portable personal computer
vocabulary.example: I work on my laptop at home.

vocabulary.meaning: A portable touchscreen computer
vocabulary.example: Kids love using a tablet for learning.

vocabulary.meaning: The main chip in a computer that runs programs
vocabulary.example: A fast processor improves performance.

vocabulary.meaning: Random Access Memory — temporary storage for running programs
vocabulary.example: Adding more RAM will speed up your computer.

vocabulary.meaning: The capacity to save files and data on a device
vocabulary.example: I need more storage for my photos.

vocabulary.meaning: A device used to recharge a battery
vocabulary.example: Don't forget your phone charger.

vocabulary.meaning: Connecting to a network without physical cables
vocabulary.example: The printer is wireless — no cables needed.

vocabulary.meaning: A short-range wireless technology for connecting devices
vocabulary.example: Pair your headphones via Bluetooth.

vocabulary.meaning: Wireless internet connection
vocabulary.example: Connect to the wifi network in the office.

vocabulary.meaning: A location offering wifi access or a phone used as a router
vocabulary.example: Use your phone as a hotspot.

vocabulary.meaning: To watch or listen to media online without downloading
vocabulary.example: I stream music on Spotify every day.

vocabulary.meaning: A pause in streaming when data is loading
vocabulary.example: The video keeps buffering due to slow internet.

vocabulary.meaning: A name used to identify yourself on a system
vocabulary.example: Enter your username and password to log in.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of entering your credentials to access a system
vocabulary.example: Click the login button to access your account.

vocabulary.meaning: The right to control who sees your personal information
vocabulary.example: Review your privacy settings regularly.

vocabulary.meaning: Security software that blocks unauthorized network access
vocabulary.example: The firewall blocked the malicious traffic.

vocabulary.meaning: Software designed to detect and remove malware
vocabulary.example: Install antivirus software on your computer.

vocabulary.meaning: Software designed to damage or gain unauthorized access
vocabulary.example: The computer was infected with malware.

vocabulary.meaning: A scam to steal personal data via fake emails
vocabulary.example: Beware of phishing emails from unknown senders.

vocabulary.meaning: Converting data into a coded format to prevent unauthorized access
vocabulary.example: Your messages are protected by encryption.

vocabulary.meaning: A security process requiring two forms of verification
vocabulary.example: Enable two-factor authentication for added security.

vocabulary.meaning: A software application for a phone or computer
vocabulary.example: Download the banking app from the store.

vocabulary.meaning: Programs and operating systems used by a computer
vocabulary.example: The software needs to be updated.

vocabulary.meaning: The physical components of a computer
vocabulary.example: The hardware upgrade improved speed significantly.

vocabulary.meaning: The main software that manages a computer's resources
vocabulary.example: Windows and macOS are popular operating systems.

vocabulary.meaning: A program used to access the internet
vocabulary.example: Chrome is a popular web browser.

vocabulary.meaning: A website used to search for information online
vocabulary.example: Google is the most used search engine.

vocabulary.meaning: To set up a program on a device
vocabulary.example: Install the update to fix the bug.

vocabulary.meaning: Replacing something with a newer or better version
vocabulary.example: I need to upgrade my laptop's memory.

vocabulary.meaning: To find and fix errors in a computer program
vocabulary.example: The developer spent hours debugging the code.

vocabulary.meaning: Temporary data stored for faster access
vocabulary.example: Clear the cache to free up space.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer that provides data to other computers
vocabulary.example: The server went down for maintenance.

vocabulary.meaning: Information processed or stored by a computer
vocabulary.example: Back up your data regularly.

vocabulary.meaning: A set of rules or steps used to solve a problem
vocabulary.example: Social media feeds use an algorithm.

vocabulary.meaning: The part of software you see and interact with
vocabulary.example: The app has a simple and clean interface.

vocabulary.meaning: An object in orbit used for communications and GPS
vocabulary.example: GPS relies on satellite signals.

vocabulary.meaning: Computer systems that simulate human intelligence
vocabulary.example: Artificial intelligence is changing many industries.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer-generated simulated environment
vocabulary.example: Virtual reality headsets are becoming popular.

vocabulary.meaning: The practice of protecting systems from digital attacks
vocabulary.example: Cybersecurity is a top priority for all companies.

quiz.question: What do you do to get an app from the internet?
What is a captured image of your screen?
What is a copy of data stored for safety?
What is used to protect your account?
What do you do when fixing system problems?
